summary: Applied two code-review fixes. (1) Replaced both single-rAF scroll patterns in HealthRecordsSection with double-rAF to ensure the scroll offset is computed after React has committed the newly-opened <details> element to the DOM — one in the mount-only hash effect and one in the registerScrollToHealthSection callback. (2) Wrapped registerScrollToHealthSection in useCallback with an empty dependency array in PetDetail.tsx so it is a stable reference and does not retrigger the registration effect in HealthRecordsSection on every parent render. Added useCallback to the existing React import. TypeScript compilation: clean.
